content.problemStatement:
For a positive integer mmm, let's define a function f(m)f(m)f(m) as the sum of all perfect powers which divide mmm. We call a positive integer kkk a perfect power if there are integers xxx and yyy such that k=xyk = x^yk=xy and y>1y \gt 1y>1.

You need to calculate the sum f(1)+f(2)+…+f(N)f(1) + f(2) + \ldots + f(N)f(1)+f(2)+…+f(N). Since the answer might be quite big, compute it modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9 + 7109+7).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the sum modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9 + 7109+7).
